evidence: Arsenic was an existing venture-backed media company (since ~2015-2016) described in press as 'a Playboy competitor for the Snapchat generation,' known for crowd-sourced Snapchat 'takeovers' where models take over the account for a day. It had over a million Instagram followers and Playboy reportedly offered to buy it. Shaan was referencing this already-real company, not proposing a new one.

OnlyFans, a subscription content platform later dominated by adult content, was founded in November 2016 by Tim Stokely -- well before the Feb 2020 episode. The 'Patreon for adult content' framing describes an already-real, already-well-known company at the time of the pitch.

Lucy Goods, Inc. was founded around 2016 by John Coogan, Samy Hamdouche, and David Renteln (Soylent alumni) and launched its nicotine gum product in 2019, raising a $10M Series A around that time -- all predating the Feb 2020 mention. No evidence found tying Sam Parker directly to Lucy as founder/investor; it appears Sam was citing an existing company as an example rather than something he/Shaan built.

Tophatter, the 90-second declining/rising 'auction flash sale' app, was founded in 2011 by Ashvin Kumar and Chris Estreich, well-established (reportedly ~$100M revenue run-rate by 2017) long before this 2020 episode. Shaan was discussing a real, already-existing company.

Bring a Trailer began in 2007 as a car blog and became a full auction marketplace by 2014, long predating the Feb 2020 episode; it's a well-known, already-real classic/collector car auction platform (BaT). Shaan referenced it as an existing model, not a new pitch.

DoNotPay was founded in 2015 by Joshua Browder, originally to contest parking tickets via a chatbot, and by 2019 had added a 'Free Trial Card' feature to auto-cancel free trials -- both features predate the Feb 2020 episode. This is a reference to an existing, already well-known company.

Everlywell was founded in 2015 by Julia Cheek, appeared on Shark Tank in 2017, raised a $50M round in 2019, and by the Feb 2020 episode already offered 30+ at-home lab tests -- a well-established, already-real company being cited as the example.

At-home STI/STD testing with telemedicine follow-up already existed via companies like myLAB Box, founded circa 2013-2014 by Lora Ivanova in Los Angeles, marketed as the first nationwide at-home STD testing platform with free telemedicine consults for positive results -- well predating the Feb 2020 episode. The guest was likely discussing this or similar existing services rather than proposing a wholly new company.

Everlywell (founded 2015) already sold an at-home finger-prick food sensitivity test (IgG reactivity to 96/204 foods) well before Feb 2020, making this a reference to an already-real product/company rather than a new idea.

The concept of combining a brewery/coffee shop with a kids' play space is a real but highly fragmented small-business trend (many independent local venues such as play cafes and breweries with playgrounds), not a single scaled company traceable to this MFM pitch. No evidence Sam/Shaan or their orbit built a venture around this, and no single named company emerged from it as a direct result of the episode.
